text: Laparoscopic exploration reveals a fistula between the terminal ileum and sigmoid colon in a 52-year-old woman with Crohn's disease.

The surgeon performs careful dissection of the fistula between the terminal ileum and sigmoid colon using a combination of sharp and blunt techniques, focusing on the terminal ileum side, which will be part of the removed specimen.

The surgeon approximates the defect in the sigmoid mesocolon using intracorporeal sutures. A flexible sigmoidoscopy is performed to visually inspect the sigmoid colon for any full-thickness defects and to conduct an air-water leak test.

The surgeon develops the retrohalial plane and completes the ileocecal resection using a medial approach. The hepatic flexure is fully mobilized from below, with clear identification of the duodenum, pancreas, and gallbladder through the medial window.
The surgeon performs an end-to-end ileocolic anastomosis on the chronically dilated small bowel, using hands-on techniques to connect the ileum and colon.
